name: Manta
summary: Manta is a sunny city on Ecuador's Pacific coast, famous for its huge tuna fishing fleet and long sandy beaches. Right next door is Montecristi, the small town where the world's real Panama hats are woven by hand.
funFacts: Panama hats are not actually from Panama at all. They are woven from toquilla straw in Montecristi, just up the road from Manta.
Manta is one of the biggest tuna fishing ports in the whole world, and you can watch boats unload fish onto the beach at sunrise.
The forest at Pacoche is full of howler monkeys whose deep roars can travel more than a mile through the trees.
Isla de la Plata is nicknamed 'the poor man's Galapagos' because you can see blue-footed boobies and frigatebirds there.
Every year from June to September, huge humpback whales swim past Manta to have their babies in the warm Pacific waters.
thingsToDo: Splash in the waves and build sandcastles at Murcielago, Manta's main city beach.
Visit Montecristi nearby and watch weavers turn straw into a real Panama hat.
Walk the forest trails of Pacoche Wildlife Refuge to spot howler monkeys and bright birds.
Run on the soft white sand of Los Frailes beach inside Machalilla National Park.
Take a boat to Isla de la Plata to meet blue-footed boobies waddling on the cliffs.
Watch paragliders launch off the cliffs at Crucita and float over the ocean.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Giant ocean animal that swims to Manta's coast each winter to have babies.

wildlife.name: Blue-footed Booby
wildlife.description: Funny seabird with bright turquoise-blue feet that does a silly dance.

wildlife.name: Magnificent Frigatebird
wildlife.description: Black seabird where the male puffs up a huge red balloon on his throat.

wildlife.name: Mantled Howler Monkey
wildlife.description: Furry tree-dwelling monkey whose loud roar carries for miles through the forest.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in Spanish, the language people speak in Ecuador.

images.alt: The long sandy curve of Murcielago Beach, Manta's main city beach on the Pacific.
A finely woven toquilla straw Panama hat made by hand in Montecristi, Ecuador.
Tall trees of the Pacoche cloud forest reserve near Manta where howler monkeys live.
A paraglider soars above the cliffs and beach at Crucita, Ecuador.
The white sand and turquoise water of Los Frailes beach in Machalilla National Park.
Visitors and a guide on Isla de la Plata, Ecuador's seabird island off Manta's coast.
A humpback whale leaping out of the ocean.
A blue-footed booby standing on a rock at Isla de la Plata, showing its bright blue feet.
A male magnificent frigatebird puffing out his bright red throat pouch.
A mantled howler monkey carrying its baby on its back in the rainforest.
